topic.what: Complete passive attack on Hecke-KE key-exchange scheme via Hecke eigenbasis decomposition
topic.how: Diagonalizes Hecke algebra over explicit number field; reduces shared key recovery to d scalar divisions; per-session cost O(d²) independent of pool size r and Hecke factors s
topic.why_matters: Hecke-KE was proposed as post-quantum key exchange; this paper proves it is unfixable and broken across entire design space

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of parameter sets where shared secret is recovered exactly
characteristics.evidence: Verified against all paper parameter sets (N=23,37,53,61,97); all recovered keys satisfy K'=K
characteristics.context: All 5 benchmark parameter sets from original paper

characteristics.evidence: B=O(kN) is Sturm bound; d=dim Sk(Γ0(N)); dominant step is solving B×d linear systems over number fields
characteristics.context: Independent of r and s; only depends on (N,k)

characteristics.evidence: 3 matrix-vector products, 2d scalar divisions, d scalar multiplications, 1 matrix-vector product; total 4d²+3d
characteristics.context: Independent of pool size r and number of Hecke factors s; verified experimentally for d≤7

characteristics.evidence: For prime N: d=g(X0(N))=genus of modular curve; d=Θ(N) asymptotically
characteristics.context: Security requires d=Ω(2^(λ/2)) for λ-bit security; honest key size Ω(d) rationals prevents implementation

characteristics.evidence: Original paper assumed integer coordinates; actual coordinates are rationals (e.g., -11339499027488009352028160/3 for N=53); bit-length substantially exceeds paper's bound
characteristics.context: Makes scheme unimplementable for security parameter λ=128 since requires Ω(2^64) bits (>10⁹ terabytes)

characteristics.what_itasures: Wall-clock time for eigenbasis computation in SageMath 10.7
characteristics.context: One-time public computation; dominates per-session attack for small d

not_for: recovering secret factorization (attack doesn't need it)

novelty: First complete break of Hecke-KE protocol
Proof that attack cost independent of pool size r and Hecke factors s
Theorem proving scheme is unfixable within its design framework
open_problems: Efficient implementation for larger d (d>7) using specialized number field libraries
Achieving practical security would require d=Ω(2^64) rational coordinates
Developing secure post-quantum key exchange based on non-commutative algebras
Investigating whether non-simultaneously-diagonalizable algebras could provide secure key exchange
